---
title: "Editing Document Classes"
---

---
title: "Editing Document Classes"
source: "https://support.hyland.com/r/Current/Hyland-Capture/ovf1646248503902"
section: "Hyland Capture"
---

To edit a document class:

1.  From the Hyland Experience Capture Administration user interface, click the Classes tab.
2.  Select the document class to be edited.
-
To filter the list of document classes displayed, enter in the Search field a string of characters contained in the name of the document class you want to select.

-
To change how the list is organized, select the appropriate option from the Sort By drop-down list.

The configurable settings for the document class are displayed.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
3.  Edit the Display Name for the document class. You may edit the Description for the document class as well.  Note: The Description is only displayed when viewing the document class in the Administration interface.
4.  To add additional bar code profiles to the document class, click Add Profile. The Add Bar Code Classification Profile dialog box is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
5.  Select the bar code profiles to add to the document class.
6.  Click Add Profile after selecting the appropriate bar code profiles. The bar code profiles are added to the document class.
7.  To edit a bar code profile associated with the document class:
1.   Click the bar code profile name.
2.  In the Bar Code Profile: <Profile Name> dialog box, click Go To profile. You are redirected to the Bar Code Profiles tab, and the configuration options for the corresponding bar code profile are displayed.
3.  Edit the profile and click Save. For more information, see Editing Bar Code Profiles.
8.  To remove a bar code profile from the document class:
1.  Click the delete icon ![](data:image/gif;base64,) corresponding to the bar code profile you want to delete.
2.  Click Remove to remove the bar code profile from the document class.
9.  To reposition a bar code profile assigned to the document class, click to the left of the name of the bar code profile and drag the field to a new position.
10.  To add additional fields to the document class, click Add Fields. The Add Fields dialog box is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
11.  Select the fields to add to the document class. To select all fields displayed, select the Field option at the top of the list.
12.  Click Add Field after selecting the appropriate fields. The fields are added to the document class.
13.  To edit a field associated with the document class:
1.   Click the field name.
2.  In the Field: <Field Name> dialog box, click Go to Field. You are redirected to the Fields tab, and the configuration options for the corresponding field are displayed.
3.  Edit the field and click Save. For more information, see Editing Fields.
14.  To remove a field from the document class:
1.  Click the delete icon ![](data:image/gif;base64,) corresponding to the field profile you want to delete.
2.  Click Remove to remove the field from the document class.
15.  To reposition a field assigned to the document class, click to the left of the name of the field and drag the field to a new position.
16.  To edit the bar code extraction configuration:
1.  Click Configure Field beside the appropriate field.
1.  In the Field: <Name> dialog box, from the Assign Extraction Profile drop-down list, select the bar code profile to be assigned to the field for extraction.
1.  To remove a bar code extraction configuration, in the Field: <Name> dialog box, from the Assign Extraction Profile drop-down list, select No Profile Assigned.
17.  Click Save.
